_doc: Type-role PERSONALITY registry (theme schema v2, Layer 1). Each theme.json carries a `typePersonality`; the composer's _theme_type_roles() executor looks up the recipe here, resolves the font slots against THAT theme's own --font-display-en/--font-body/--font-mono, and emits the --eyebrow-*/--hero-num-*/--display-*/--stat-label-*/--caption-* vars on #root. A theme's own tokens.css still overrides (it is injected AFTER this), so the two ported exemplars keep their hand-tuned values. Same-personality themes look different because their font families differ. Font slot ∈ {display, body, mono}. Roles + property names must match the block executors in compose.py (honesty-tested by tests/test_type_roles.py + validate_themes.py).

geometric-sans.desc: clean geometric grotesque — a tight-tracked sans eyebrow, upright bold sans numerals, mono captions (Swiss / consulting / keynote register)

editorial-serif.desc: upright serif magazine — a mono eyebrow, upright serif numerals, mono captions (newsroom / print / literary)

elegant-italic.desc: italic serif gallery — a quiet mono eyebrow, ITALIC serif numerals + display, wide mono captions (the vellum/dark-botanical signature)

mono-technical.desc: engineering / terminal — a wide-tracked mono eyebrow + labels, upright display numerals (blueprint / terminal / cyber / voltage)

brutalist-heavy.desc: black-display manifesto — a heavy tight sans eyebrow, ultra-bold numerals, heavy body labels (bauhaus / signal / neubrutalism)

friendly-rounded.desc: soft / approachable — a body-font eyebrow, rounded bold numerals, sentence-warm labels (pastel / split / chalk)
